clinical_workflow.intended_use: Clinician-controlled documentation scaffold for sedation recovery documentation.
clinical_workflow.not_for: Not for autonomous diagnosis, treatment selection, dosing, disposition, admission/discharge, procedural, imaging, referral, or emergency management decisions.

source_phase1.reason_for_inclusion: Common Anesthesia / Perioperative Medicine documentation scenario included for structured clinician-controlled notes; safety labels are conservative because it must not generate management advice.
source_phase1.distinct_purpose: Distinct high-caution documentation workflow for clinician-stated assessment and plans around sedation recovery documentation, without generating management advice.
source_phase1.duplicate_check_notes: Cleaned Phase 1 candidate. Exact ID is unique within this revised index and must be checked against existing_workflow_ids.json and alias inventory before import; no close baseline workflow assigned in this cleaned index.

safety_notes: No autonomous diagnosis or treatment recommendation is intended.
Red flags are documentation prompts only.
High-risk specialty/workflow: documentation scaffolding only; requires conservative medical review before use.
Marked not_suitable_yet pending Hossam medical review and technical gating.
red_flag_prompts: airway, aspiration, allergy, bleeding, or cardiorespiratory risk documented if assessed
consent/risk discussion documented only if completed by clinician
perioperative concern documented without management advice

workflow_identity.evidence_relationship: The ASA standard supports a documented recovery record and ongoing evaluation of oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain, nausea, hydration, and other observed domains without auto-populating findings.

The ASA standard supports a documented recovery record and ongoing evaluation of oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain, nausea, hydration, and other observed domains without auto-populating findings.

chips.evidence_relationship: The BSG section supports monitoring until return to baseline and documented recovery only in gastrointestinal endoscopy sedation; it is not generalized to all sedation procedures.

history_draft.text: Patient presents for sedation recovery documentation documentation. [Onset/duration]. [Symptom context]. [Associated symptoms reviewed]. [Relevant negatives]. [Patient concerns/goals]. [Additional history].

exam_prompts.text: Documented if assessed or measured. Pre/perioperative assessment documentation documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Airway assessment documentation if assessed documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Cardiorespiratory assessment documentation documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Risk discussion documentation documented.

investigation_options.text: Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Preoperative assessment results documented.

plan_options.text: Risk/consent discussion documented if completed by clinician.

clinical_blockers: Procedure, sedation type, medicine, dose, oxygen, airway, respiratory and cardiovascular observations, consciousness, pain, nausea, temperature, events, intervention, response, baseline recovery, score, discharge decision, escort, instructions, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
